title: Password secured landing page
excerpt: Hi, looking for an app which allows creating simple page (something like to-do list would be ideal) based on data from monday.com board. I just want to share this one-pager with customers, so they could check updates and…

posts.author: mateuszmlyniec (Mateusz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, looking for an app which allows creating simple page (something like to-do list would be ideal) based on data from monday.com board. I just want to share this one-pager with customers, so they could check updates and/or see what is still missing in the project we do for them.

I do not want to give each customer access or even view-only access to the monday board, I’m looking for something more friendly-looking in terms of one-pager/landing-page/to-do-list and separated from monday.

posts.author: 0xm3t1 (Flynn .Is)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

You might want to check out the monday.com Marketplace — there’s likely an app that fits your use case, or at least something close to it.

If you don’t find exactly what you need and this is important for your workflow, feel free to DM me. We can explore creating a custom app tailored to your vision.

Best,

posts.author: MASO (Markus @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @mateuszmlyniec

We got you covered with two potential apps that could be of interest for you.

Board to Website Widget lets you create a share- and embeddable one-pager from a board. Though there is no interaction for the user, and you would have to care about password protection on your behalf, if this is mandatory for you. Find some live demos and examples here.

Client Portal Builder follows a way more advanced approach (which might be out of scope for your use case), and lets you build a password protected portal displaying board data tailored to each client along with interaction features. Learn more here or explore our demo portal from a clients perspective [Email: perry@portal.com | Password: #demoportal25].

Let me know what you think.

– Markus

posts.author: mateuszmlyniec (Mateusz)
posts.cookedHtml: Markus, that looks great. How many dashboards/websites I can built within one paid account?

posts.author: sameer1 (Muhammed Sameer Sheikh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey, If you’re really looking for a solution like this, I would recommend connecting with these guys.

They helped me sort my issue of same sort as well by creating a portal for me that works. Contact: saad@protogroup.co

posts.author: MASO (Markus @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
@mateuszmlyniec, there is no limit for published widgets. Though it’s one widget per board (main table), and the total amount of automations per month is shared across all widgets (in case you want to auto-publish them periodically). A 14-day free trial comes along with the app, to test around and see if it’s a fit for your requirements.

– Markus
